RESULTS DOC

Outputs for the ASCENT-03 historical ECA.

Study Details

Study element Description
Study title Characteristics and Outcomes of Patients with Metastatic Triple-Negative Breast Cancer (mTNBC) Treated with First-line TPC Chemotherapy as a historical External Control Arm for ASCENT-03 Trial: A Retrospective Observational Cohort Study
Protocol ID GS-US-593-7865
Study period December 18th 2010 - June 30th 2025
Patient identification period January 1st 2011 - April 22nd 2020
RW cohort data cut-off date June 30th 2025
ASCENT-03 data cut-off date April 2nd 2025 [Primary Analysis]
DARS ID 2025-ONC-077
DARS filepath //solasdata/cds_obs_heor/data_analytics/2025/2025-ONC-077/
GitHub respository https://github.com/Gilead-CDS-RWE/2025-ONC-077–A03-ECA/
Redshift data schema name flatiron_cc_breast_ascent_eca_raw_20251011


Attrition table - RW cohort

No. Eligibility criteria N remaining N excluded % remaining of previous step
0 Total patients in Flatiron Health Custom Data Solution for ASCENT-03 External Control Arm - Primary Cohort 491 NA NA
1 mBC diagnosis on after January 1, 2011 491 0 100.0%
2 Aged 18+ at mBC diagnosis 491 0 100.0%
3 Evidence of HER2 negative, ER negative, and PR negative and the absence of positive test for HER2 or ER or PR within +/- 60 days of mBC diagnosis 489 −2 99.6%
4 First line of mTNBC treatment confirmed as either paclitaxel, nab-paclitaxel, or carboplatin and gemcitabine, and initiated on or before December 31, 2023 472 −17 96.5%
5 No other primary malignancy within 5 years before mBC diagnosis 450 −22 95.3%
6 No participation in a clinical trial during the index treatment 450 0 100.0%
7 Initiated first line of mTNBC treatment before SG approval date in mTNBC (April 22, 2020) 288 −162 64.0%
8 Exclude if ECOG >=2 on the closest record to index date in the period 90 days pre-index date up to and including 7 days post index date 245 −43 85.1%
9 Exclude if evidence of inadequate hematologic or organ function in the period 30 days pre-index date up to and including the index date 210 −35 85.7%




BROAD RW COHORT

  • “Broad Cohort” = all eligibility criteria prior to the application of “trial-like” ECOG and adequate hematologic/organ function.
  • “Fully-Specified Cohort” = all eligibility criteria including ECOG and adequate hematologic/organ function.

Time to Death/Last Alive from 1L
N 288 210 78
Mean (SD) 17.8 (20.3) 19.8 (20.6) 12.5 (18.7)
Median (IQR) 11.4 (5.3-21.0) 13.0 (7.5-23.5) 6.2 (2.4-14.3)
Min-Max 0.0-125.6 0.3-125.6 0.0-110.1

¹ Insurance types are not mutually-exclusive. ² All patients where stage was 'Not documented' have at least 3 months between initial and metastatic diagnosis. ³ Sites of metastasis recorded any time before index date until 30 days after index date (inclusive); sites are not mutually exclusive. ⁴ Closest ECOG to index date, in the period 90 days pre-index date until 7 days after index date (inclusive). ⁵ Closest weight to index date, in the period 30 days pre-index date until 7 days after index date (inclusive). ⁶ Closest PD-L1 result to index date, from the start of the study period until 30 days after index date (inclusive). ⁷ HER2 expression within +/-60 days of metastatic diagnosis date. ⁸ BRCA1/2 test result recorded from start of study period until 30 days after index date (inclusive). Patients with variant of unknown significance (VUS) were considered negative. ⁹ Hepatic impairment was defined as 'Normal' if bilirubin ≤ ULN and AST ≤ ULN; 'Mild' if ULN < bilirubin ≤ 1.5×ULN; 'Moderate' if 1.5×ULN < bilirubin ≤ 3×ULN; 'Severe' if bilirubin > 3×ULN.

¹ Insurance types are not mutually-exclusive. ² All patients where stage was 'Not documented' have at least 3 months between initial and metastatic diagnosis. ³ Sites of metastasis recorded any time before index date until 30 days after index date (inclusive); sites are not mutually exclusive. ⁴ Closest ECOG to index date, in the period 90 days pre-index date until 7 days after index date (inclusive). ⁵ Closest weight to index date, in the period 30 days pre-index date until 7 days after index date (inclusive). ⁶ Closest PD-L1 result to index date, from the start of the study period until 30 days after index date (inclusive). ⁷ HER2 expression within +/-60 days of metastatic diagnosis date. ⁸ BRCA1/2 test result recorded from start of study period until 30 days after index date (inclusive). Patients with variant of unknown significance (VUS) were considered negative. ⁹ Hepatic impairment was defined as 'Normal' if bilirubin ≤ ULN and AST ≤ ULN; 'Mild' if ULN < bilirubin ≤ 1.5×ULN; 'Moderate' if 1.5×ULN < bilirubin ≤ 3×ULN; 'Severe' if bilirubin > 3×ULN.

rwOS - Basic Adjustments for SG




ASC-03 OUTCOMES

  • Results from A-03 are included for internal information only
  • Primary analysis data cut-off was April 2, 2025
  • Interim analysis 1 data cut-off was October 27, 2025
  • The hazard ratios with 95% CI presented in the OS and PFS plots below were calculated using Cox proportional hazards models stratified by the stratification factors at randomization
  • All results have been verified against the published primary results or the Interim Analysis Topline TFLs

Weighting Approaches

Approach Description/Variable selection
A Sex, Age at randomization/index date, Time from diagnosis to mBC with de-novo indicator, indicators of sites of metastasis at baseline (Bone, Brain, Liver, Lung)
B Same variables as Approach A + pre-randomization treatment of physician’s choice chemotherapy regimen (either Gem/Carbo, nab-Pac, or Pac)
C Same variables as Approach A + Race (weighting failed)
D Same variables as Approach A; weighting performed after first removing n=15 SG-treated patients from RW ECA
E Same variables as Approach A + imputed baseline ECOG
F Same variables as Approach B + imputed baseline ECOG
Subgroup Split by TPC. Same variables as Approach A, except without Sex in Gem/Carbo and Paclitaxel subgroups because all pts were female in either the ECA or ASCENT-03 data
Trim @ XX Large weights were capped (winsorized) at the 95th percentile weight (no observations were excluded)


Weighting Results

  • “HR” = Hazard ratio of ASCENT-03 SG Arm vs. the approach in the row, computed by Cox proportional hazards model
  • “Median” = Median time-to-event, computed by Kaplan-Meier
  • For Approaches E and F involving multiple imputation:
    • No attempt was made to pool median time-to-event
    • Weights diagnostics tables are provided per imputation in the WEIGHTS DIAGNOSTICS section
Approach
Overall survival
Progression-free survival
Weights diagnostics

WEIGHTED KM PLOTS - OS

  • Trimmed-weight versions are not presented here, but are stored in the DARS study folder
  • Approach C is omitted (model included Race)
  • Kaplan-Meier plots for Approaches E and F were not created (approaches included multiple imputation)
  • All OS plots have been truncated at t=60 months; all PFS plots have been truncated at t=36 months
  • ‘N at risk’ table is omitted because in the wECA, this represents a weighted count rather than a count of physical patients

Approach A


Approach B


Approach D


TPC Subgroups

Gemcitabine/Carboplatin


nab-Paclitaxel


Paclitaxel




WEIGHTED KM PLOTS - PFS

  • Trimmed-weight versions are not presented here, but are stored in the DARS study folder
  • Approach C is omitted (model included Race)
  • Kaplan-Meier plots for Approaches E and F were not created (approaches included multiple imputation)
  • All OS plots have been truncated at t=60 months; all PFS plots have been truncated at t=36 months
  • ‘N at risk’ table is omitted because in the wECA, this represents a weighted count rather than a count of physical patients
  • Plots showing both trial arms and wECA are included for additional context and to demonstrate similarity in PFS between weighted external cohort and actual trial control arm

Approach A

ASCENT-03 SG Arm vs Weighted ECA


ASCENT-03 SG Arm vs ASCENT-03 Control Arm vs Weighted ECA
